criteria.label: Explain histogram-based split finding in your own words.
criteria.example: Write 2–3 sentences: bin numeric feature → sum gradients per bin → try splits at bin edges only → pick best gain.

criteria.label: Contrast leaf-wise and level-wise tree growth with one library example each.
criteria.example: LightGBM leaf-wise picks max-gain leaf (num_leaves); XGBoost level-wise splits all leaves per depth (max_depth).

criteria.label: Train XGBoost or LightGBM with validation early stopping and report best iteration.
criteria.example: Fit XGBClassifier with eval_set and early_stopping_rounds=30; print model.best_iteration and validation AUC at that round.

criteria.label: Set a sensible baseline hyperparameter block for a 100k-row tabular classifier.
criteria.example: List: learning_rate=0.05, num_leaves=31 or max_depth=6, subsample/feature_fraction=0.8, early_stopping_rounds=30.

criteria.label: Describe how missing values are handled in-tree vs upfront imputation.
criteria.example: In-tree: learned default branch per split. Imputation: fill before training — compare val metrics on same split.

criteria.label: Link this topic to Gradient Boosting, Random Forests, Missing Values, and Tracking Metrics.
criteria.example: One sentence each: boosting loop elsewhere; RF bagging baseline; imputation comparison; log val metric per round.
